text: Which freight elevator condition is treated as part of a limited access space exception?
option_a: A destination-oriented elevator with hall call consoles
option_b: A freight elevator that is not part of an accessible route
option_d: Any passenger elevator on an accessible route

text: Elevator pits and elevator penthouses are examples of what kind of spaces?
option_b: Dwelling units required to be Type B
option_c: Equipment spaces frequented only by service personnel
option_d: Destination-oriented elevator lobbies

text: Where no elevator service is provided or required, Type B unit requirements are limited to which units?
option_a: Only units above the lowest story
option_b: All units on every story of the structure
option_c: Only multistory units with internal stairs
option_d: Only units on the qualifying accessible stories

text: At least one story containing residential dwelling or sleeping units must have what accessibility feature?
option_a: An accessible entrance and accessible route from the exterior
option_b: A LULA elevator serving all floors
option_d: A freight elevator connected to the lobby

text: When other residential stories are served by a qualifying building entrance, what must be Type B units?
option_a: Only units on stories above the entrance
option_b: All dwelling and sleeping units served by that entrance on that story
option_c: Only the first unit closest to the entrance
option_d: Only units served by a private residence elevator

text: How is a multistory dwelling or sleeping unit without elevator service treated for Type B unit requirements?
option_b: Only its upper floor must comply
option_c: It is not required to be a Type B unit
option_d: It must always be a Type B unit

text: If a multistory unit has external elevator service to only one floor, what must that floor be?
option_b: Excluded from Type B requirements
option_d: The primary entry to the unit and compliant with Type B requirements

text: Where elevator service provides an accessible route only to the lowest residential story, which units must be Type B?
option_a: Only units on the lowest story served by the elevator
option_b: No units because elevator service is limited
option_c: Only units on stories above the elevator landing
option_d: All units in the building

text: In buildings without elevator service where Type B+NYC units are required, which stories control the required units?
option_b: The qualifying cellar, basement, or first-floor story
option_c: Only stories served by destination dispatch
option_d: Every story above grade

text: Passenger elevators on an accessible route must comply with ICC A117.1 and what other chapter?
option_b: Chapter 16 of the code
option_d: Chapter 9 of the code

text: For passenger elevators on an accessible route, which ICC A117.1 requirement is not applied?
option_b: Platform lift provisions
option_d: Arrangement requirement for elevator car controls

text: Which elevator system triggers the special accessibility rules for hall call consoles and responding cars?
option_b: Private residence elevators only
option_d: Freight elevators not on an accessible route

text: For destination-oriented elevators, which group of hall call console requirements must be checked?
option_b: Elevator chapter installation rules only
option_d: Number and location, required features, and arrangement

text: Where must hall call consoles be located in relation to the elevator landings they serve?
option_a: Accessible routes connecting to the elevator landings they serve
option_b: Only inside the elevator car
option_d: Any wall within the elevator machine room

text: When hall call consoles are built into security barriers, where must at least one console for each type of use be located?
option_a: On any floor above the lobby
option_b: On an accessible route connecting to the served elevator landings
option_c: At the elevator machine room only
option_d: Inside the security office

text: On transfer floors, sky lobbies, and floors containing building entrances, where is at least one wall-mounted hall call console required?
option_a: At the top of each stair
option_b: At the remote security desk only
option_c: Adjacent to each elevator hoistway entrance
option_d: Inside each elevator car

text: On floors other than transfer floors, sky lobbies, and entrance floors, how many hall call consoles are required?
option_a: One total console for the entire building
option_b: One for every ten elevators on the roof only
option_d: One for every five elevators, or fraction thereof, on each side of the elevator landing

text: What projection or recess range applies to hall call consoles that are not flush with surrounding surfaces?
option_b: Minimum 1/8 inch and maximum 1/2 inch
option_d: Exactly 48 inches

text: What must activation of the accessibility function button start?
option_b: The required audio output
option_d: The freight elevator mode

text: What minimum height is required for the visible identification symbol on an accessibility function button?
option_b: 1/4 inch
option_d: 3/4 inch

text: When must audio output begin for the accessible hall call console function?
option_a: Opening of the elevator machine room door
option_b: Operation of the fire alarm only
option_d: Activation of the accessibility function button

text: How quickly must audio output commence after the accessibility function button is activated?
option_b: Within one minute
option_d: Within ten seconds

text: What maximum audio volume applies when measured 36 inches horizontally from the source?
option_b: 80 dB
option_d: 60 dB

text: What contrast ratio is required for display characters, numbers, and symbols against their background?
How long must elevator assignment characters and numbers remain displayed?
option_b: One second
option_d: Five seconds

text: When a responding elevator car answers an accessibility-function call, what must it provide during travel in addition to audio output?
option_a: An in-car announcement of all floors on which it stops
option_b: A printed receipt of the selected floor
option_c: A visual-only confirmation at the lobby
option_d: A shutdown of the hall call console
